level_signals: 5+ years of full-cycle SaaS sales experience
Proven record of meeting or exceeding annual revenue targets in a high-growth environment
OTE range $200K–$400K indicating senior individual contributor
Expected to present to C-suite executives

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of full-cycle SaaS sales experience, ideally with enterprise-level clients
Proven record of meeting or exceeding annual revenue targets in a high-growth environment
Skilled at complex sales cycles, tailoring messaging to different buyer personas and decision makers
Proven success outbound prospecting to large enterprise
Exceptional written and verbal communication; comfortable presenting to C-suite executives
Adept at identifying client pain points and mapping solutions that drive business value
Ability to collaborate effectively with both cross-functional teams and technical stakeholders

scope.surface: Perplexity Enterprise Pro — full-cycle enterprise sales from prospecting to onboarding
primary_metrics_inferred: Monthly and quarterly individual revenue targets
core_responsibilities: Drive new business by prospecting, pitching, and closing Perplexity Enterprise Pro solutions with large organizations
Diligently research prospects, develop account strategies, and execute outreach campaigns tailored to company and industry needs
Conduct high-impact product demonstrations and respond to RFPs and technical inquiries
Lead client engagement through the entire sales cycle—from initial presentation, technical evaluation, negotiation, to closing and onboarding
Build and nurture senior-level relationships across enterprise clients, championing Perplexity's value proposition and customer outcomes
Collaborate closely with Product, Marketing, and Customer Success to ensure customer satisfaction and solution fit
Maintain up-to-date knowledge of Perplexity's platform, industry trends, and competitor landscape
Meet or exceed individual and team revenue targets on a monthly and quarterly basis
Maintain accurate records of pipeline and account activity using CRM tools

compensation.equity_form: equity may be part of the total compensation package

green_flags: OTE range explicitly disclosed ($200K–$400K), indicating competitive compensation
Comprehensive benefits including health, dental, vision, and 401(k) stated
Early-stage enterprise team ('small but mighty') suggests high impact and ownership potential
Clear product and market focus (enterprise AI / Perplexity Enterprise Pro)
red_flags: No location or remote/hybrid policy mentioned anywhere in the JD — unclear where the role is based
Very wide OTE range ($200K spread) makes it difficult to gauge where a given candidate would land
No mention of visa sponsorship or work authorization
No preferred/nice-to-have criteria listed, making it harder to differentiate qualifications beyond the baseline

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from OTE range and years requirement)
scope.team_size_estimate (inferred from 'small but mighty Enterprise team')
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and SaaS sales context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities, not stated explicitly)
compensation.base_range_usd (not stated; only OTE range provided)
location.type (not stated anywhere in the JD)
ambiguities: The JD states $200,000–$400,000 as OTE but does not break out base vs. variable components, making it impossible to extract a base salary range.
No location is specified in the JD; it is unclear whether this role is remote, hybrid, or onsite and in which city.
The seniority qualifier 'ideally with enterprise-level clients' on the 5+ years requirement softens what is otherwise framed as a required qualification — treated as hard given surrounding context.
